name: Class-A Amplifier
description: Single NPN BJT Class-A amplifier with voltage-divider bias. Transient simulation at 1kHz. Optimizes output swing, distortion, and efficiency. Demonstrates large-signal nonlinear behavior.

parameters.name: R_load
parameters.label: Collector Load Resistor
parameters.description: Collector load resistor (sets quiescent current and gain)

parameters.name: R_emitter
parameters.label: Emitter Degeneration Resistor
parameters.description: Emitter degeneration resistor (bypassed by Ce for AC)

metrics.name: Output_Swing_V
metrics.label: Output Voltage Swing
metrics.description: Peak-to-peak output voltage swing (limited by supply and bias)

metrics.name: THD_percent
metrics.label: Total Harmonic Distortion
metrics.description: Harmonic distortion as percentage of fundamental

metrics.name: Efficiency_percent
metrics.label: Power Efficiency
metrics.description: AC output power / DC input power (Class-A max ~25%)

class_a_requirements.comment: For maximum symmetric swing, V_CE should be near VCC/2

design_constraints.name: active_region
design_constraints.description: Quiescent V_CE must have headroom from both saturation and cutoff

design_constraints.name: bias_current
design_constraints.description: Quiescent collector current should be 0.5mA to 20mA

design_constraints.name: thermal_limit
design_constraints.description: Power dissipation should be under 500mW for small-signal transistor

saturation_clipping.description: Very low swing indicates transistor hitting saturation during signal swing

cutoff_clipping.description: Low swing with high distortion indicates clipping at cutoff

excessive_distortion.description: THD above 50% indicates severe nonlinearity or clipping

efficiency_anomaly.description: Class-A efficiency above 25% is theoretically impossible

bias_failure.description: Near-zero swing indicates transistor is biased off or in deep saturation
